summary: Reverse shell to hardcoded remote host on module load
details: paperclip-adapter-helpers@1.0.12 contains top-level code in dist/server/index.js that spawns a detached shell executing python/bash reverse-shell one-liners connecting to 185.112.147.174:7007. The spawn fires unconditionally when the module's `./server` export is loaded (e.g., when Paperclip's adapter loader requires it), giving the operator of that remote host an interactive shell on the installer's machine. Additionally, package.json declares the name `paperclip-adapter-helpers` while the README documents the package as `vps-new-manager` and instructs users to install it through Paperclip's adapter installer — a name/README mismatch consistent with impersonation of the Paperclip adapter ecosystem to lure installs.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
